items.headline: City updates children and youth services master plan
items.summary: San José City Council received a status report on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan, along with updates on youth empowerment programs, the Safe Summer Initiative, and youth intervention services. This could signal new or expanded programs affecting families with children in the city.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan reviewed
items.summary: The city presented a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. This may include plans for library improvements, renovations, or changes to services that affect public library users across San José.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing delayed, date TBD
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8 and rescheduled for a future date to be announced. Residents interested in this development should watch for a new notice when the hearing is rescheduled.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to check meeting minutes for details on what this construction project entails and where it will be located in Los Gatos.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail traffic signal project advances
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This ongoing traffic safety project may affect commuters and residents near the Bascom Avenue corridor.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is developing an updated Active Transportation Plan that affects how the city prioritizes biking and walking infrastructure. Phase 2 includes edits to the plan and new scoring methods that will determine which projects get funded next.

items.headline: Council considers changes to vacancy process for appointed positions
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, potentially changing the process for appointing new members when seats open up. This could affect how quickly the council can fill empty positions and who has input on replacements.

items.headline: 65-unit senior housing complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium Senior Apartments will add nearly 8.8 million dollars of senior housing with 65 units at 678 E St John St. This addresses growing demand for age-restricted housing in San José's tight rental market.
